_meta.comment: Flat dotted-key namespace. Use {placeholder} syntax for variables. Add new languages by copying this file and translating values; keys must match exactly.

welcome.intro_lead: Welcome. Before you begin, three things worth knowing.
welcome.first_body: this tool listens before it speaks. The first few minutes are an honest diagnostic: what you bring, where you are, what you need. The recommendation you receive comes from your answers, not from a template.
welcome.second_body: the game is scaffolding. It exists to help you keep the practice when the practice is young and the mind is distractible. When the scaffolding is no longer needed, set it down.
welcome.third_body: whatever your tradition or teacher, this is a complement, not a replacement. If your practice is already deep, use this lightly. If you are beginning, use this as a companion — but the real teacher is the sit itself.
welcome.privacy_eyebrow: Your data · what you should know
welcome.privacy_local: Everything you write — reflections, journal entries, diagnostic answers, what you name as your struggles — stays on this device, in this browser's local storage. Nothing is ever sent to a server. The app has no backend and no account system.
welcome.privacy_export: You can export it all to a file at any time in Settings. If you clear your browser data, use private browsing, or uninstall, the data is gone — so export regularly if you care about keeping it.
welcome.privacy_outbound: The only things that leave this device are: links you tap to SuttaCentral (a public sutta archive), and beta feedback you explicitly send as email. Both require your action.
welcome.takes_3_minutes: Takes about 3 minutes to set up.
welcome.one_line: A meditation companion rooted in the Theravāda tradition.
welcome.sign_in_link: I already have an account — sign in
welcome.footer_privacy: Private by default. Your data stays on your device.
_section: Common UI labels — shared across many call sites.
_section_setup_level: Setup — Step 3, choose your quest level.
setup.level.intro: Choose the level that matches where you actually are. Tap to select, then Continue. Be honest — Sotāpatti is honorable. Most practitioners overestimate.
_section_setup_habit_mode: Setup — choose how sit duration behaves over the quest.
setup.habit_mode.heading: Choose Your Habit Mode
setup.habit_mode.subheading: How should the duration of your sits behave over the path?
setup.habit_mode.constant.title: Constant
setup.habit_mode.constant.body: Your duration stays the same throughout the path. Locked in. Consistency, not escalation, is the way.
setup.habit_mode.constant.quote: "The Buddha taught the same breath instructions to beginners and arahants. The depth changes; the form stays."
setup.habit_mode.progressive.title: Adaptive & Progressive
setup.habit_mode.progressive.body: Hit your current duration 7 days in a row, and the system offers a small bump (+3 to +5 minutes). You can always decline. Growth at the pace your practice can hold.
setup.habit_mode.progressive.quote: "The Buddha called the path 'gradual training' — anupubbasikkhā — never sudden, always one supportable step at a time."
setup.habit_mode.custom.title: Custom Duration
setup.habit_mode.custom.subtitle: (set with sliders)
setup.habit_mode.custom.body: Pick exactly how long each sit should be. Locked at your chosen values for the path.
setup.small_habits.heading: Supporting Habits
setup.small_habits.intro_singular: Pick {maxLabel} habit to support your sit. The sit is the foundation. These are companions.
setup.small_habits.intro_plural: Pick {maxLabel} habits to support your sit. The sit is the foundation. These are companions.
setup.assessment.phase_a.heading: Before we begin
setup.assessment.phase_a.intro: The Buddha met each practitioner where they were. So will this tool. The assessment has three short parts and takes about three minutes. No grades, no wrong answers shown.
setup.assessment.wellbeing.intro: Meditation work can surface difficult material — grief, old trauma, strong emotion. For most practitioners most of the time, this is part of the work. But for some states — active psychosis, acute suicidal crisis, untreated severe trauma, recent bereavement still raw — meditation on your own is not the right container. A qualified professional is. This tool cannot substitute for that, and it is not a failure of practice to need a human first.
setup.assessment.wellbeing.ok_title: I'm in a steady enough place to do this work
setup.assessment.wellbeing.ok_desc: Not perfect — just not in acute crisis. Ordinary life difficulty is welcome here.
setup.assessment.wellbeing.struggling_ti: I'm struggling, but I want to keep going — gently
setup.assessment.wellbeing.struggling_de: The engine will keep sits short, emphasize kindness practices, and recommend the softer suttas first.
setup.assessment.wellbeing.crisis_title: I'm in real distress — I need a human first
setup.assessment.wellbeing.crisis_desc: We will pause the setup and point you to resources. You can come back any time.
setup.assessment.crisis.lead: Thank you for being honest. The most helpful thing right now is a living person, not an app. Please reach out to one of these:
_note_crisis_resources: Localization hot spot. Translators should reorder/extend these 5 lines for their country. Each entry is one <li> with flag + resource name + contact. Keep the HTML tags.
setup.assessment.crisis.resource_iasp: 🇬🇧 International Association for Suicide Prevention — directory of crisis lines by country: iasp.info/resources/Crisis_Centres
setup.assessment.crisis.resource_person: A trusted friend, family member, or doctor — just one person.
setup.assessment.crisis.immediate_danger: If you are in immediate danger, please call your local emergency number (112 in Europe, 911 in the US, 999 in Malaysia).
setup.assessment.crisis.dhamma_note: The dhamma has been waiting 2,500 years. It can wait another week or month. Take care of the immediate first. When you come back, this setup will still be here, and we will begin gently.
setup.assessment.phase_a.pause_button: Pause setup — I will come back
setup.assessment.phase_b.sub_beginner: A few questions to set you up for comfort and a realistic start. About a minute.
setup.assessment.phase_b.sub_experienced: Deeper questions. Including a no-score knowledge check so the engine can tell whether the foundation is solid or whether it should surface early. About two minutes.
_section_setup_assessment_phase_c: Setup — Phase C: five hindrance sliders.
setup.assessment.phase_c.heading: The hindrances, right now
setup.assessment.phase_c.intro: Five sliders. The canonical map the Buddha gave — nīvaraṇā, the coverings. Honest answers make the recommendation more useful.
_section_setup_rec_header: Setup — recommendation card (post-assessment). This is the emotionally weighty moment where the engine reveals what it heard.
setup.rec.heading: What the engine recommends
setup.rec.intro: Based on what you shared. A starting point, not a verdict. You can accept it, adjust it, or set your own. The path is yours to walk — this is only orientation.
_section_setup_rec_hindrance_names: Dominant-hindrance display names. Keyed individually so verify_strings catches each usage (no dynamic keys). 'default' is used when no hindrance is selected.
setup.rec.first_sutta_note: Available in the Wisdom tab after the vow. You will be pointed to it on day one.
setup.rec.supporting_note: Not required. Offered because your experience level suggests they will support, not burden.
_section_setup_rec_beginner_care: Setup — rec card — beginner-care block (shown to new practitioners).
setup.rec.beginner_care.label: For your first sits
setup.rec.beginner_care.reassurance: What you said you were worried about
_section_setup_rec_knowledge_check: Setup — rec card — knowledge reveal for regular/long practitioners. Perfect/partial/miss branches per category.
setup.rec.knowledge.label: Your knowledge check — what was named
setup.rec.knowledge.intro: Not a quiz result. A mirror held up quietly. Most long-sitting practitioners have gaps here; it is how this tradition is usually taught. The teacher notices, offers the reading, and moves on.
setup.rec.knowledge.truths_perfect: All four named, nothing added. This is a solid foundation — the engine will not need to circle back here.
setup.rec.knowledge.truths_missed: Not marked: {names}. The four are: dukkha, its origin (craving), its cessation, and the eightfold path. SN 56.11 has them in the Buddha's own first-sermon form.
setup.rec.knowledge.truths_wrong: Adjacent teachings were folded in: karma, rebirth, compassion, the three characteristics — all real and important, but not members of the specific list called the Four Noble Truths. The precision matters because the fourth truth is the path.
setup.rec.knowledge.eight_perfect: All eight named, nothing added. Good.
setup.rec.knowledge.eight_missed: Factors not marked: {names}. The eight fall into three groups — wisdom (right view, right intention), ethics (right speech, right action, right livelihood), and concentration (right effort, right mindfulness, right concentration). MN 141 lays them out fully.
setup.rec.knowledge.eight_wrong: Things picked that are not members of the eightfold path: {names}. Faith (saddhā), devotion, and compassion are real and central to the dhamma — but as faculties, attitudes, or brahmavihāras, not path factors. A small clarification, worth knowing.
setup.rec.knowledge.engine_adjusted: The engine has quietly adjusted your first weeks to lean foundation-ward. No penalty is being carried forward. This is how teaching works.
_section_setup_rec_truth_names: Four Noble Truth display names. Keyed individually so truth lookups survive verify_strings.
_section_setup_rec_insights: Setup — rec card — insights block (purple border, optional sutta link).
setup.rec.insights.label: Foundation observations
setup.rec.insights.intro: What your answers reveal — not as judgment, as orientation. Each one points to something the engine will keep in view.
setup.rec.adjust.label: The minutes already set by what you told me
setup.rec.adjust.intro: Adjust only if today feels genuinely different from what you said. Most practitioners overestimate; the recommendation tends to be the honest version.
setup.rec.adjust.tail: If you leave the recommendation alone, the teacher has chosen. Most practitioners overestimate. Starting small is honorable. You can always change this later in Settings.
setup.rec.validation.zero_sits: All three sits are currently set to zero. A meditation tracker needs at least one sit to track. Set the morning, midday, or evening sit to at least five minutes — the shortest honest sit is still honorable.
_section_setup_care_pause: Crisis-path pause modal (shown when user selects 'real distress' in Phase A wellbeing check and taps pause).
setup.care_pause.heading: Take the care you need
setup.care_pause.body: The setup has been paused. No practice was started, nothing was saved. When you feel ready — in a week, a month, whenever — come back, and we will begin together.
setup.care_pause.tail: The dhamma does not go anywhere. It is always waiting.
_section_setup_welcome: Setup — Step 0: the welcome narrative shown before anyone chooses anything (ehipassiko frame + three steps + honesty disclaimer). Separate from renderWelcome (the pre-setup landing page).
setup.welcome.version_suffix: the app listens first, recommends second, teaches third
setup.welcome.ehipassiko_body: You are about to begin a practice modeled on the Buddha's own path to awakening. You will face Māra's ten armies — the same forces the Bodhisatta faced beneath the Bodhi tree. You will collect wisdom from the suttas as you walk.
setup.welcome.ehipassiko_italics: The Buddha called this ehipassiko — come and see for yourself. Nothing here is to be taken on faith. Test it against your own experience. Keep what reveals itself; set aside what does not.
setup.welcome.three_steps_header: The three steps before you begin
setup.welcome.step1_body: Your name, and a character from the canon — Sāriputta, Moggallāna, Ānanda, or another — whose archetype will anchor you. Each one has an ability; pick the one whose pattern you want to strengthen in yourself.
setup.welcome.step2_title: The teacher listens before it teaches.
setup.welcome.step2_body: A short assessment — about three minutes — maps your energy, experience, the hindrances pulling at you right now, and your current understanding. No grades. No right-or-wrong shown. The engine uses what you say (and what you miss) to set a realistic starting point.
setup.welcome.step3_title: The path is named. You take the vow.
setup.welcome.step3_body: The engine offers a duration, a focus, and a first sutta. You can accept, adjust, or overrule. Then a single formal line — the vow — and the walking begins.
setup.welcome.honesty_lead: A word of honesty before you begin.
setup.welcome.honesty_body: This tool is for beginners and intermediate practitioners — those still forming the habits, those who need structure to keep showing up. It is not a substitute for a living teacher, for a sangha of flesh-and-blood friends, or for professional mental-health care when that is what is needed. If you are currently in crisis, in active psychiatric distress, or recently bereaved — the most compassionate thing is to seek a qualified person, and come back later. This is serious work. The dhamma is the path; this is only a small bridge.
setup.welcome.nothing_written_note: You can close this at any time — nothing is written to your device until you take the vow in Step 3.
_section_setup_mode: Setup — Step 1: story-path vs. free-path choice.
setup.mode.heading: Do you want the story, or just the tracker?
setup.mode.subheading: The dhamma is the same in both. The difference is only whether the app frames your practice as a path with characters and Māra's armies — or stays out of the way and lets you run your own system.
setup.mode.story_body: A guided path. The engine listens, sets a realistic duration and focus, picks your first sutta, and walks the Māra-armies arc with you. Characters from the canon stand in for the patterns you are strengthening.
setup.mode.free_body: A bare tracker. You define every habit and duration yourself. No assessment, no Māra, no stages, no recommendations. For practitioners who already know what they need and want the minimum scaffolding.
setup.mode.switch_later: You can switch later in Settings — but the path you pick now shapes the next three or four screens.
setup.character.eyebrow: Step 1 · Name yourself. Choose a companion.
setup.character.heading: Who is walking?
setup.character.bridge_body: In the suttas, the Buddha's closest disciples each embodied a different facet of the path. Sāriputta was foremost in wisdom and analytical insight. Moggallāna in psychic power and deep concentration. Ānanda in memory and loving attention. Kassapa in austere restraint. Pick the one whose pattern you want to strengthen in yourself — not the one you already are. The character is not an avatar. It is a reminder, every day, of the quality you are cultivating. Each one grants a small ability inside the game, chosen to fit the archetype.
setup.character.walk_alone_note: For now, you walk alone. Later versions will allow inviting others to a real sangha.
setup.character.choose_hint: (tap any card to read their story)
setup.character.tap_to_select: Tap a character card above to select
_section_setup_vow: Setup — Step 7: the vow ceremony. Every word matters — this is the moment of commitment before walking begins.
setup.vow.heading: The Vow
setup.vow.subheading: Your path has been set. Confirm to begin.
setup.vow.minutes_move_body: The game adapts. If you miss the key habit three times in a seven-day window, you'll be offered a compassionate downgrade — no penalty, no shame. If you hold the practice for a full week, you'll be offered a gentle increase. Your discipline is not predicted; it is listened to.
setup.vow.bodhisatta_quote: "Let only skin and sinew and bone remain. Let the flesh and blood dry up. I will not rise from this seat until I have attained what is to be attained."
setup.vow.bodhisatta_attribution: — The Bodhisatta's vow beneath the Bodhi tree (MN 36 — Mahāsaccaka Sutta)
_section_setup_custom_finish: Setup — renderSetupCustomFinish. Free-path closer (shorter flow — no assessment, no recommendation).
setup.custom_finish.heading: Free Path Setup
setup.custom_finish.subheading: You'll add habits manually after setup. No path, no story, no Shadow.
setup.custom_finish.after_setup_note: After setup, go to the Habits tab to add your own habits. The Shadow mechanic is disabled in Free Path mode.
_section_study_overview: Study tab — main render. The SRS-powered sutta deck view where daily review happens.
study.no_member: Select a practitioner to see study cards.
study.due.body_one: 1 sutta with material to revisit. The spaced-repetition engine picks each card at the moment your memory of it is beginning to soften — that's when review locks the material in. A short session is enough.
study.due.body_many: {n} suttas with material to revisit. The spaced-repetition engine picks each card at the moment your memory of it is beginning to soften — that's when review locks the material in. A short session is enough.
study.empty.heading: All current cards are resting
study.empty.body_prefix: The engine has spaced your existing cards out to their next review dates. Good.
study.empty.tail_no_active: Start a new sutta below to begin building your study deck.
study.empty.tail_has_active: You can still open any sutta below to practice ahead of schedule — but you don't need to.
study.header.heading: 🎴 Study
study.header.body: This is how the old oral tradition worked — the suttas were memorized through repeated recitation, not casually read once. The modern equivalent is spaced repetition: each teaching gets revisited at widening intervals, so it lives in you rather than slipping through.
study.header.note: Tap any sutta to open its card deck. Rate each answer honestly — the engine schedules your next review based on how well you knew it. No XP, no grades. Just the quiet work of holding the teaching.
study.read.eyebrow: You have read these — ready to study?
study.read.heading_one: 1 sutta read, study not yet begun
study.read.heading_many: {n} suttas read, study not yet begun
study.read.body: Reading the sutta once opens the door. Study turns it into a card deck — 4-8 questions per sutta, scheduled by the engine so you actually remember the teaching in a month, a year, five years.
study.library.eyebrow: The full library — grouped by teaching
study.library.heading: Browse by category
study.library.body: Each group below contains the suttas the tradition turns to for that area of practice. A locked sutta is above your current rank — not withheld as punishment, just that the Buddha sequenced the teaching for a reason.
study.library.locked_alert: This sutta opens at rank {n}. The tradition teaches these in order; the earlier ones build the frame for this one.
_section_study_groups: Library group labels. Used in renderStudy's library section to label each teaching-category of suttas.
study.footer_note: Spaced-repetition intervals: box 0 → same day · 1 → tomorrow · 2 → 3 days · 3 → 1 week · 4 → 2 weeks · 5 → 1 month · 6 → mastered. A card rated "hard" drops back one box.
_section_srs_modal: SRS study modal — the flashcard review UI triggered from the Study tab or Begin Review Session button.
srs.modal.session_done_one: Done for today. You reviewed 1 card in this session.
srs.modal.session_done_many: Done for today. You reviewed {n} cards in this session.
srs.modal.nothing_due: Nothing due right now. Your cards are scheduled — they'll return when due.
srs.modal.rate_prompt: How did that match what you knew?
srs.modal.rate_honest_note: Honest self-rating. The scheduler is only useful if you tell it the truth.
srs.modal.recall_prompt: Take a moment. Recall what you know — then reveal the check.
_section_wisdom_modal: Wisdom modal — scroll-detail view. Triggered when a wisdom scroll is tapped (in sidebar, stage-advance list, etc.). Uses the existing scroll-reveal animation.
_section_army_challenge: Army-challenge modal — tap an army card on the dashboard. Shows the canonical army description (from MARA_ARMIES data) and a challenge prompt. The army data itself lives in content/mara-armies/ — only the UI chrome needs t().
_section_army_defeated: Army-defeated celebration modal — shown when the 'I Defeated' button is tapped or after meeting the three-day gate.
army.defeated.heading: Army Defeated
army.defeated.shadow_recedes_one: The Shadow recedes by 8. 1 army remains.
army.defeated.shadow_recedes_many: The Shadow recedes by 8. {n} armies remain.
_section_stage_advance: Stage-advance modal — big moment when a stage completes and the next begins. Shows new scrolls unlocked + the next stage's description.
stage_advance.heading: Stage Complete
_section_victory: Victory modal — the final Awakening ceremony. Emotionally weighty; words matter. The Buddha's quote is preserved from MN 26 — translators should use their tradition's canonical rendering if one exists, else translate faithfully.
victory.heading: Awakening
victory.buddha_quote: "Done is what had to be done. There is nothing further for this world."
victory.buddha_attribution: — The Buddha's first words after liberation, MN 26
victory.body_1: You have walked the Bodhisatta's path. You faced Māra's armies and did not turn back. You sat through the watches of the night.
victory.body_2: But the dhamma reminds us: the awakening of one is also the beginning. The Buddha, after his liberation, hesitated at first to teach — wondering if anyone could understand. Then he saw beings with little dust in their eyes, and walked to Sarnath to share what he had found.
victory.body_3: Your XP is now permanent. Begin a new path when you are ready.
_section_wisdom_tab: Wisdom tab — main render. Nested collapsible sections: meditation guidance, foundations, struggle finder, canonical library, codex.
wisdom.heading: 📜 Wisdom
wisdom.intro: The teaching material, the meditation guidance, and the scrolls collected on the path. The Path tab is for the quick visual view of where you are. This tab is for the heavy reading — tap any section below to expand it, then tap anything inside to go deeper.
_section_wisdom_guidance: Wisdom tab — meditation guidance container (first of two top containers).
wisdom.guidance.heading: 🧘 Meditation guidance
wisdom.guidance.expanded_note: The practices themselves. Start with the basics; the deeper teachings open as you go.
wisdom.guidance.collapsed_note: Tutorial, guided flows, and the four foundations of mindfulness.
wisdom.guidance.tutorial_desc: Posture, breath, the first ten minutes. Start here if you are starting.
wisdom.guidance.four_foundations_label: Mindfulness of the four foundations · MN 10 · MN 118
wisdom.guidance.four_foundations_intro: The Buddha's own map. Each foundation is a separate gate into the same room — the room of what is actually happening in experience right now.
wisdom.guidance.foundation_body_desc: — mindfulness of posture, of the breath (ānāpānasati, MN 118), of the body's parts, of the elements
wisdom.guidance.foundation_feeling_desc: — pleasant, unpleasant, neutral — before the story
wisdom.guidance.foundation_mind_desc: — the quality of mind itself: contracted, spacious, clinging, releasing
wisdom.guidance.foundation_dhammas_desc: — hindrances, aggregates, sense bases, awakening factors, four noble truths
_section_wisdom_foundations: Wisdom tab — foundations container (second of two top containers). Four Noble Truths, Eightfold Path, three layer chips, three list sections.
wisdom.foundations.heading: ☸️ Foundations
wisdom.foundations.expanded_note: The map of the whole teaching. Read this when the context of your practice needs placing.
wisdom.foundations.collapsed_note: Four Noble Truths · Eightfold Path · Five Hindrances · Seven Awakening Factors · Māra's Ten Armies.
wisdom.foundations.four_truths_title: The Four Noble Truths & Eightfold Path
wisdom.foundations.four_truths_desc: SN 56.11 — The first sermon. The structure underneath everything.
wisdom.foundations.five_hindrances_list_: The five hindrances · tap to read
wisdom.foundations.seven_factors_list_la: The seven awakening factors · tap to read
wisdom.foundations.mara_armies_list_labe: Māra's ten armies · tap to read (Layer 2 reference)
_section_wisdom_struggle: Wisdom tab — struggle finder (sutta-by-life-topic browser). Group labels + sub labels are data in content/sutta-taxonomy/ — translators edit those separately; only the chrome here lives in en.json.
wisdom.struggle.heading: 🧭 Find a sutta for your struggle
wisdom.struggle.expanded_note: The Buddha gave specific teachings for specific situations. When life is hard, when a question arises, when a habit won't shift — there is usually a sutta for it. Tap a topic to see what he taught.
wisdom.struggle.collapsed_note: Sutta finder by life topic: relationships, work, death, mental states, practice struggles.
_section_wisdom_library: Wisdom tab — canonical library. Tag-labels are UI taxonomy (translatable), Pali terms included per tradition. Sutta content itself lives in content/suttas/ markdown files.
wisdom.library.heading: 📚 The full canonical library
wisdom.library.expanded_note: All {total} suttas, organized by what they cultivate. Each has a minimum rank — at or above your rank, reading earns paññā. Below your rank, reading is allowed but costs paññā and ticks today's sensual-desire diagnostic. The dhamma is not gatekept — but reaching for what you are not ready for has a real price.
wisdom.library.collapsed_note: All {total} suttas with rank gating. For the card-deck view of what you've studied and what's due, see the Study tab.
wisdom.library.reading_log_count: {read} of {total} suttas in your reading log
wisdom.library.bottom_note: Reading the sutta means looking it up in your translation (Bhikkhu Bodhi recommended), reading the actual text, then returning here to mark it as read. The summaries in this tool are paraphrases to help you decide what to read — they are not the suttas themselves.
_section_wisdom_library_tags: Wisdom library — tag labels (sutta-subject taxonomy). Each includes the Pāli term inline; translators should preserve the Pāli where traditional.
_section_wisdom_codex: Wisdom tab — codex (collected wisdom scrolls). Celebratory collect-them-all view.
wisdom.codex.heading: 📜 The Codex — collected teachings
wisdom.codex.expanded_note: Short teachings unlocked at milestones along your chosen path. They are paraphrases of canonical sources — tap any to read, then look the original up in the suttas.
wisdom.codex.collapsed_note: {collected} of {total} collected · {sealed} sealed at later milestones.
wisdom.codex.empty: No teachings collected yet. They reveal as you pass path milestones.
wisdom.codex.sealed_note: Hidden until you reach the corresponding milestone — this keeps later teachings from confusing beginners.
_section_path_tab: Path tab — renderPath. The main rank/progress view with rank header, tisikkhā bars, focus card, hindrances grid, awakening factors grid, Layer 2 preview, visual ladder, and the 'your work right now' two-column card.
path.rank_header.rank_9_tail: From here, rank advancement stops. The next transition — into the ariya tiers — is beyond what any self-report tool can certify. Keep practicing. Find a teacher in the living tradition.
path.tisikkha.note: All three trainings must reach their threshold AND the telemetric requirements must be met for the next rank to be reached.
_section_path_focus: Path focus card. The returned object from getFocusForNow() is localized there so renderPath just reads .title, .subtitle, .detail etc. Three modes: release (when a hindrance dominates), cultivate (when a factor lags), deepen (default — sati).
path.focus.release_detail: {pali} is the hindrance moving strongest in your data over the last 14 days. The work is not to fight it but to recognize it. Each time it arises in the sit, name it silently — "{pali} is present" — and watch it as weather, not as you.
path.focus.cultivate_detail_tail: Each sit and each named-hindrance journal entry strengthens this factor directly.
path.focus.deepen_subtitle: {english} — the foundation of every other factor
path.focus.deepen_detail: No hindrance is dominating and no factor is lagging. The next step is to deepen mindfulness — the foundation that supports the entire path. Watch closely; name precisely; do not rush.
_section_path_hindrances_card: Hindrances grid on the path tab. Status values (quiet/weakening/present/strong/no data) are stable IDs returned by getHindranceRemovalProgress — each gets its own display key.
path.hindrances.eyebrow: ⟵ What is being abandoned · tap for Layer 1 detail
path.hindrances.heading: The Five Hindrances
path.hindrances.subtitle: Pañca nīvaraṇāni · MN 10 · AN 5.51
path.hindrances.intro: Each hindrance has its own removal progress. Tap any to read more. The bar fills as the rolling 14-day average drops. None of these need to be uprooted — only reliably suppressed so that practice is not derailed.
_section_path_factors_card: Awakening factors grid on the path tab.
path.factors.eyebrow: ⟶ What is being cultivated · tap any factor to read
path.factors.heading: The Seven Awakening Factors
path.factors.subtitle: Satta Bojjhaṅgā · SN 46 · MN 118
_section_path_layer2_preview: Layer 2 preview card on the path tab — shows Māra's Ten Armies, either as read-only context (before unlock) or as engagement UI (after unlock).
path.layer2.heading: Māra's Ten Armies
path.layer2.subtitle: Sn 3.2 · the deeper defilements · tap header for Layer 2 detail
path.layer2.not_active_body: Layer 2 unlocks when Layer 1 (the five hindrances) has been reliably suppressed for at least one full day. Until then, the ten armies are readable for context — tap any to learn what each one is — but no progress is being tracked. This is by design: the deeper work cannot begin until the preliminary work has held.
path.layer2.intro: After the five hindrances are reliably suppressed, the deeper defilements become workable. These are the ten armies the Buddha faced under the Bodhi tree. You will work with them one at a time — not all at once.
_section_path_ladder: Path ladder card — visual SVG ladder of the 10 ranks.
path.ladder.eyebrow: The path of liberation · ten ranks · tap any checkpoint
path.ladder.footer_note: Ranks 0–5 are the preliminary work — walked through the five hindrances and the first visible awakening factors. Ranks 6–9 are the ariya tier: game-achievable milestones through Māra's ten armies, but explicitly not real attainment claims. The game ends at rank 9 with a liberation ritual that hands you a final teaching summary. Tap any checkpoint to see what it is and what you need to reach it.
_section_path_tail: Cloud simile tail card + link to the full path detail modal.
path.tail.cloud_simile: The cloud simile: the hindrances and defilements are weather passing through the sky of mind. The sky is not damaged when clouds pass through it. The damage comes from believing you are the clouds.
path.tail.full_detail_link: open the full path detail · trends · export →
_section_path_your_work: The 'your work right now' two-column card (release · cultivate). The primary digest: what the data currently says is your single most pressing hindrance and your weakest measurable factor.
path.your_work.default_antidote: Notice it as it arises. Name it. Let it pass.
path.your_work.release_no_data: No diagnostic data yet. Tap the daily diagnostic to begin tracking the hindrances.
path.your_work.cultivate_tail: The weakest measurable factor — strengthen it through deliberate practice this week.
path.your_work.cultivate_no_data: Awakening factor data still building. Continue sitting and reflecting.
path.your_work.footer_note: The two threads of practice. Set as your sit-intention; check at evening close.
path.details_collapsible: ▸ Show full focus + all hindrances + all awakening factors
_section_rank_intro_modal: First-time rank introduction modal. Fires ONCE before the very first rank_announcement. Contextualizes the whole rank-of-the-path system so the first announcement isn't disorienting. Emotionally weighty — introduces canonical Pali categories (puthujjana, sotāpanna, etc.) with the strong disclaimer that ranks are descriptive, not attainment claims. Translators: the canonical Pali terms should be preserved; their translations in the tradition may already exist in the target language's Buddhist vocabulary.
rank_intro.eyebrow: A note before the system introduces itself
rank_intro.heading: About the "rank of the path"
rank_intro.body_1: The Theravāda tradition names stages a practitioner moves through. The canonical names — puthujjana (worldling), sotāpanna (stream-enterer), sakadāgāmī, anāgāmī, arahant — describe what has fallen away from the mind, not what has been achieved. Before these, commentaries describe finer sub-stages: andha-puthujjana (the uninstructed worldling), kalyāṇa-puthujjana (the well-trained worldling who has heard the teaching and begun sīla), and so on.
rank_intro.body_2: This tool will, from time to time, reflect back a rank based on your telemetry — sits held, reflections written, hindrance averages, gate conditions met. This is descriptive, not an attainment claim. The game says "you currently look like a well-trained worldling in the phase of establishing concentration"; it cannot say whether a fetter has fallen away, and it will not try.
rank_intro.body_3: For the four noble stages — sotāpatti onward — the tool gives the game version of the rank with an explicit note every time: "no self-report system can verify this; that work is yours and your teacher's." The purpose is orientation, not medals. The game rank may go up or down as practice changes.
rank_intro.shadow_body: As your rank rises through the first five stages, Māra's pressure (what the game calls the Shadow) also rises — not as a punishment, but because the practice is working and his territory is being threatened. The Shadow floor peaks just before stream-entry — "most practitioners give up here" — and then falls with each noble stage. You may feel hindrances more strongly in the middle of the path; that is the design. The announcement cards will explain each transition when it happens.
rank_intro.caveat_note: If any of this feels grandiose or ego-inflating, set the ranks aside and watch only the hindrance averages. The ranks are never required reading.
_section_rank_announcement_modal: Rank-announcement modal. Fires on every rank advancement. Includes the dynamic 'floor changed' teaching — a nine-entry canonical dictionary of rank transitions. Each transition's teaching is emotionally weighty and doctrinally specific (Shadow rising through preliminary ranks, peaking at rank 5, falling through the ariya stages). LOCALIZATION HOT SPOT — the floor-transition teachings (floor_teaching_5_6 through floor_teaching_8_9 especially) describe canonical Buddhist attainments. Translators should prefer their tradition's established vocabulary for sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī/anāgāmī/arahant and the fetters.
rank_announcement.closing_note: This rank is a reflection of what the practice is doing, not something the practice is for. The work is the same: sit, reflect, see clearly. The rank may go up or down as that work continues.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_0_1: You have begun the path. Māra notices. The floor of his pressure has risen from 20 to 30. You may feel the hindrances more strongly now than when you began — this is not failure, this is the practice working. You have become visible.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_1_2: Sīla is establishing. The floor rises to 40. The first real friction with Māra's pull begins now.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_2_3: Concentration is developing. The floor rises to 50. Māra applies more pressure as your mind learns to gather.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_3_4: Right view is forming. The floor rises to 60. Now Māra has reason to fight.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_4_5: You are approaching the threshold. The floor is now at 70 — its maximum. This is the most dangerous rank in the entire path because most practitioners give up here, expecting the work to ease and finding instead that it intensifies. This is the night under the Bodhi tree for any practitioner. Stay. The floor will fall after stream-entry.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_5_6: Rank 6 reached in the game. Māra's pressure floor falls from 70 to 60 — in the tracker, this reflects sustained work across the three-legged gate. The canonical teaching near this territory is sotāpatti-phala: the weakening of self-identity view, doubt, and clinging to rituals. The game does not claim that attainment; it reports the work. What actually fell away is yours and your teacher's to verify.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_6_7: Rank 7 reached. Māra's floor drops further — from 60 to 40 — reflecting consistent engagement with three or more of his armies. The canonical parallel is sakadāgāmī-phala: the tradition describes sensual desire and ill-will weakening at this stage. The game reports the sustained work; whether the fetters have in fact weakened is not something a tracker can know.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_7_8: Rank 8 reached. The floor falls from 40 to 20 — the measure of deep, consistent cultivation. The canonical parallel is anāgāmī-phala, the non-returner, in whose lived experience the five lower fetters are said to have ended. The game describes what it sees in your practice. Whether that parallel holds in your own heart is yours alone — and your teacher's — to say.
rank_announcement.floor_teaching_8_9: Rank 9 reached — pariyosāna, the completion of the scaffolding. The floor falls to 5: the full teaching arc has been walked. The canonical language for full liberation — arahatta — is used nowhere as a rank name here, because no app can claim that territory. What this rank tells you is that the scaffolding has done its job. Scaffolding comes down once the building stands. The door to real practice opens here.
_section_rank_checkpoint_modal: Rank-checkpoint modal. Triggered by tapping any checkpoint on the path-map SVG. Shows that rank's canonical description + requirements list (met vs not) with appropriate status framing (walked / current / ahead).
rank_checkpoint.reqs_ahead: What you need to reach it
rank_checkpoint.progress_note: Each requirement moves closer as you sit, journal, and work with the hindrances. Nothing here is gamed by clicking — only by practice.
rank_checkpoint.rank_0_note: No requirements — this is where every practitioner begins. The uninstructed worldling, before contact with the teaching.
_section_shadow_explainer_modal: Shadow-explainer modal — reached from the Shadow stat pill or from the path tab. Explains Māra's pressure curve (floor, current, daily work) and why the floor rises to peak at rank 5 before falling.
shadow.modal.heading: The Shadow
shadow.modal.subtitle: Māra's pressure on your practice · adaptive by rank
shadow.modal.curve_label: Where you are on the curve
shadow.modal.why_floor_body_1: Māra is always present. The Shadow has a minimum at every rank — it cannot drop to zero until the game ends, and even at rank 9 it does not reach zero entirely. The canonical record (Māra-saṃyutta, SN 4) shows Māra continuing to visit the awakened Buddha until his final passing — unable to find purchase, but always trying.
shadow.modal.why_floor_body_2: The pressure scales with your threat to him. A worldling absorbed in sense-pleasure draws little attention because they are no threat. As you wake up to the path, the floor rises. It peaks at rank 5 — the threshold of stream-entry — because this is the most dangerous moment, the Bodhi-tree night for any practitioner. After breakthrough, the lower fetters fall and Māra loses real ground; the floor begins to drop.
shadow.modal.floor_note: Cannot be reduced by any amount of daily practice. Lowers only when you advance ranks — that is the canonical reward of breakthrough.
shadow.modal.daily_desc: — the gap between current and floor
shadow.modal.daily_note: This is the part that responds to today's practice. Sit and it recedes; miss a day and it grows. Your goal is to keep this near zero.
shadow.modal.harder_label: If you feel the work getting harder as you advance
shadow.modal.harder_body: This is not failure — this is right view. The hindrances arising more strongly as practice deepens means the mind has cleared enough to see what was always there. The Buddha himself describes this in MN 19. Every practitioner who has reached the stream has felt this peak. Press through. The floor will fall after rank 5.
_section_path_viewer_modal: path_viewer modal — the full detail view behind the 'open the full path detail →' link in renderPath. Three-layer breakdown (hindrances/armies/fetters), trends sparklines, member-specific rank card, factor cultivation card, and export. Largest modal in the app.
path_viewer.main_subtitle: Three layers. The game teaches each one clearly enough that you can eventually walk it without the game.
_section_path_viewer_layer1: path_viewer Layer 1 section — five-hindrance gate analysis with three-legged gate details. PATH_LAYERS[0] provides title/pali/teaser/graduation from data (content/dhamma/path-layers.json) — only chrome lives here.
path_viewer.layer1.dominant_note: A visitor, not you. Name it when it arises.
path_viewer.layer1.no_dominant: No clear dominant hindrance yet — more daily check-ins will sharpen this.
path_viewer.layer1.three_legs_label: The three legs of the gate
path_viewer.layer1.three_legs_intro: Middle way: reliable suppression, not uprooting. All three must hold for a day to count.
path_viewer.leg.composite_label: Hindrance composite ≤ {ceiling} / 10 (rolling {window}-day average across all five)
path_viewer.leg.sits_label: At least {min} sits logged in the last {window} days
path_viewer.leg.journal_label: At least {min} reflection entries that name a hindrance
path_viewer.layer1.sustained_note: A day counts when all three legs pass. A day where any leg fails resets the counter.
path_viewer.layer1.stage1_passed_note: ✓ Layer 1 gate passed on {date}. The hindrances still arise; what changed is that they no longer derail the practice.
_section_path_viewer_layer2: path_viewer Layer 2 section — Māra's Ten Armies engagement UI (when available) or read-only preview (when not).
path_viewer.layer2.engagement_intro: You work with one army at a time. Declaring engagement does not "activate" a fight — it just brings the army into focus so each day of sustained practice counts toward weakening it.
path_viewer.layer2.engaged_footer: Currently engaging: {name}. Each day the three-legged gate passes advances the engagement counter. Status transitions: engaging → weakening (7 days) → quiet (30 days).
path_viewer.layer2.not_engaged_footer: No army currently engaged. Tap "engage" on any army to begin working with it. You can only engage one at a time.
path_viewer.layer2.preview_label: The ten armies, from Sn 3.2 — preview
path_viewer.layer2.preview_note: Listed here as education, not as an active mechanic. Engagement with specific armies becomes available once Layer 1's three-legged gate has passed on at least one day — not because the game withholds them, but because the work on them is only meaningful after samādhi is steady enough that the deeper defilements become visible.
_section_path_viewer_layer3: path_viewer Layer 3 section — Ten Fetters + Four Noble Persons, preview only. Data comes from TEN_FETTERS and NOBLE_PERSONS (inline constants; translators edit those separately in the content folders).
_section_path_viewer_rank_card: path_viewer rank card — per-member current rank with full 10-rank canonical ladder visualization and next-rank requirements.
path_viewer.rank.beginning_label: You are here — at the beginning
path_viewer.rank.beginning_body: The first ranks are not about achievement, they are about showing up. The path is walked one sit at a time. Every practitioner in every tradition began exactly where you are now.
path_viewer.rank.ladder_note: The last four (Sotāpanna → Arahant) are shown as aspiration. The game does not advance to them.
_section_path_viewer_trends: path_viewer trends card — 30-day sparklines derived from rankHistory snapshots.
path_viewer.trends.empty_note: Not enough history yet — trends appear after a few days of practice. The shape of your practice takes time to show up.
path_viewer.trends.heading: How the practice is moving
path_viewer.trends.intro_note: The shape of the line matters more than any single day. A steady climb means the practice is taking root. A plateau is not failure. A dip is information, not verdict.
_section_path_viewer_cloud: path_viewer cloud simile card. Two variants — with dominant hindrance interpolated or fallback.
path_viewer.cloud.with_dom: The cloud simile: the hindrances and defilements are weather passing through the sky of mind. The sky is not damaged when clouds pass through it. The damage comes from believing you are the clouds. Naming a state as "{pali} is present" — rather than "I am this" — is already half the work.
path_viewer.cloud.no_dom: The cloud simile: the hindrances and defilements are weather passing through the sky of mind. The sky is not damaged when clouds pass through it. The damage comes from believing you are the clouds. Naming a state as "a hindrance is present" — rather than "I am this" — is already half the work.
_section_path_viewer_factors: path_viewer factors card — Seven Awakening Factors with two states (empty vs populated).
path_viewer.factors.empty_body: These are the seven qualities your practice will develop. None of them are moving yet — this is where you begin. The first factor, sati (mindfulness), begins the moment you notice a hindrance arising and name it in your journal. That is how the whole sequence starts.
path_viewer.factors.populated_intro: The positive counterpart to the hindrance work. Where the hindrances are what must be abandoned, these are what must be developed. They arise in sequence — each one supports the next. The first three are measurable from what you are already logging; the middle three emerge only when the earlier factors are established; the last one rests on all the others.
path_viewer.factors.footer_note: Pīti, passaddhi, and upekkhā are not things a self-report slider can honestly measure. They are shown as "arising" only when the earlier factors are steady enough that they could plausibly have emerged — and even then, what the game can detect is a shadow of the actual experience. Trust the cushion, not the indicator.
_section_reflection_tab: Reflection tab — main view. Daily/weekly/monthly reflection entry points, SN 22.101 adze simile, data export, weekly summary link, history counters.
reflection.heading: 🪞 Reflection
reflection.subtitle: Beyond the cushion. The whole development.
_section_reflection_adze_simile: SN 22.101 — the canonical adze simile the project is named after. Translators: this is a direct paraphrase of an iconic Theravāda simile — preferable to use the tradition's established rendering in the target language.
reflection.adze_quote: "A practitioner undertaking the development cannot see 'today my fetters wore down this much.' Like the carpenter who cannot see the daily wearing of his adze handle — but after long use sees the marks worn into the wood and knows the wearing happened. Use the adze."
reflection.daily.completed_sub: The question returns tomorrow, from a different angle.
reflection.weekly.done_body: Completed this week. You can revisit your answers any time.
reflection.weekly.available_body: Available now. Takes about 10 minutes of real reflection. Best done on a quiet day — Sunday if you have one.
reflection.weekly.cycle_complete: You have completed the twelve-week cycle. The contemplations begin again next week — but you will not be the same practitioner beginning them.
reflection.monthly.done_body: Completed this month. The contemplation remains available to revisit.
reflection.monthly.available_body: Available now, in the final week of the month. Set aside 15 minutes when you can sit without interruption. This is real practice, not a journal prompt.
reflection.export.body: Adze stores everything in this browser. To back it up, move it to another device, or just keep your reflections somewhere readable, you can export the whole thing to a JSON file and import it again later.
reflection.export.note: The export file is plain JSON — readable in any text editor. Importing replaces all current data, so export first if you want a safety copy.
reflection.summary_tile.heading: Looking back — last 7 days
reflection.summary_tile.body: Habit completion, factor trends from your daily check-ins, an honest word about the week, and a teaching from the forest. Available any time.
_section_settings_account: settings — Account & sync card. The authed / locked / local branches all route through static keys (lesson #3) so verify_strings can pair them with call sites.
settings.account.heading: Account & sync
settings.account.blurb_authed_unlocked: Signed in as {email}. Your practice data is encrypted on this device before it's sent to the server.
settings.account.blurb_authed_locked: Signed in as {email}, but the encryption passphrase isn't loaded — sync is paused until you enter it.
settings.account.blurb_local: Adze stores everything locally by default. Signing in lets you sync across devices, end-to-end encrypted.
settings.account.delete_note: Removes your account, your synced data, and the local copy on this device. Cannot be undone.
sync.cross_tab_stale_warning: Another tab updated your synced data. Reload this tab to continue.
_section_rank_ladder: rank_ladder modal footer — the tooltip shown inside the rank-ladder view. Must preserve the scaffolding-language pattern (canonical attainment is referenced, never declared).
rank_ladder.tooltip_footer: ◆ training ranks · ✦ approaching the path · ◇ ariya (preview only, never certified by the game). The game ladder ends at Rank 9, which the game labels with the canonical term for the fully-liberated mind; whether any practitioner has in fact reached that territory is only ever theirs and their teacher's to verify. Beginners are Rank 0 or 1. No one is expected to start at the top; the path is walked, not awarded.
_section_reflection_history: reflection_history modal — in-app readback of past daily/weekly/monthly reflections (v15.17). Teaching-framed as vicāra (investigation), not progress-tracking.
reflection.history_tile.heading: Your reflections
reflection.history_tile.body: Revisit what you noticed on past days — to ask whether it still applies.
reflection.history_tile.body_empty: Your evening and weekly reflections will appear here — something to return to when you want to see what you noticed.
reflection_history.heading: Your reflections
reflection_history.framing: Past observations are reminders, not records. What matters is what you notice now.
reflection_history.empty: Nothing written yet. The first reflection you write will begin your record.
daily_reflection.heading: Your teacher asks
daily_reflection.textarea_placeholder: Sit with it. One sentence is enough.
daily_reflection.privacy_note: No one sees this but you.
_section_weekly_reflection_modal: weekly_reflection modal — 12-week contemplation cycle. Context / questions / closing / teaser come from WEEKLY_REFLECTIONS data (content/reflections/weekly/*.json).
weekly_reflection.checkin_sub: Five quick sliders. These shape your teacher's guidance for next week.
_section_monthly_reflection_modal: monthly_reflection modal — 6-month deep contemplation cycle. Content from MONTHLY_REFLECTIONS data.
monthly_reflection.contemplation_label: The contemplation — sit with this before answering
monthly_reflection.checkin_sub_with_prev: Seven sliders — last month's values shown for comparison, then two short writing prompts.
monthly_reflection.checkin_sub_no_prev: Seven sliders, then two short writing prompts.
_section_evening_reflection_modal: evening_reflection modal — auto-fires after 6pm if daily reflection not yet done. Includes a 3-slider diagnostic if not yet filled + the day's reflective question.
evening_reflection.heading: Before the day closes
evening_reflection.sliders_intro: Three sliders. Your teacher watches these trends over time.
evening_reflection.textarea_placeholder: Sit with it. One sentence is enough.
evening_reflection.privacy_note: No one sees this but you.
_section_oneline_journal_modal: oneline_journal modal — quick one-line capture with hindrance-naming paññā bonus.
oneline_journal.heading: One line about today
oneline_journal.subtitle: A single sentence. No theme. No question. Whatever you noticed.
oneline_journal.hindrance_hint: Naming a hindrance by name (sensual, ill will, sloth, restlessness, doubt) earns extra paññā. But this is not required — write whatever is true.
_section_review_tab: Review tab (renderReview) — long-arc view with totals, SRS due panel, practice flavor / pre-post chips, saved quotes, weekly charts (sits / minutes / reflections / hindrance arc), and the SN 22.101 anchor teaching at the bottom.
review.no_member: Select a practitioner to see their Review.
review.heading: 📈 Review
review.intro: The long-arc view. You cannot see the adze-handle wearing day by day — but over weeks, the work shows. No streaks, no goals, no comparisons. Just what happened.
review.totals.footer_note: These numbers are descriptive, not prescriptive. The sit today is the only one that matters; this is just what has accumulated behind you.
review.srs.scheduled_body: Your cards are scheduled. They return when due — no need to chase.
review.srs.footer: Each card takes about 30 seconds. Honest self-rating only.
review.cultivated.subtitle: Not a score. A description of the practice itself — its flavor and its arc.
review.cultivated.pre_label: What I brought to the cushion
review.cultivated.post_label: What I left the cushion with
review.cultivated.arc_note: The contrast between the first list and the second is the practice. Not every sit transforms the state — and when it doesn't, that is also information. Over weeks you will see the arc.
review.quotes.subtitle: The teachings that found you. Tap the heart to unsave. These print alongside your reflections.
review.early_days_one: You have been on the path for 1 day. The weekly charts will start filling in once a full week has passed. For now, focus on the sit today.
review.early_days_many: You have been on the path for {n} days. The weekly charts will start filling in once a full week has passed. For now, focus on the sit today.
_section_review_charts: renderReview weekly charts — collapsed by default behind a toggle card. Four chart cards: sits, minutes, reflections, hindrance arc. Shared 'range label' key covers all four chart subtitles.
review.charts.toggle_note_one: this week of bar charts. Tap to open.
review.charts.toggle_note_many: last {n} weeks of bar charts. Tap to open.
review.charts.hindrance_footer: From your reflection entries. Seeing which hindrance has been dominant over time is often more useful than which one is dominant today — a visitor you see once is weather; one that keeps returning is something to name and work with.
review.anchor.quote: "A practitioner undertaking the development cannot see 'today my fetters wore down this much.' Like the carpenter with the adze handle — but after long use, the marks are worn into the wood."
_section_weekly_summary_modal: weekly_summary modal — reached from the reflection tab's 'Weekly Summary' tile. Shows 7-day habit completion, 4-branch honest-word framing, factor sparklines, reflection count, Ajahn Chah teaching. The hard-week framing references MN 36 — another localization hot spot for canonical Buddhist content.
weekly_summary.delta_same: about the same as the previous 7 days
weekly_summary.framing.no_habits: No key habits yet — once you have some, this view will fill in. The chart fills as the data does.
weekly_summary.framing.hard: This was a hard week — about {pct}% of your key habits completed. The path makes room for hard weeks. The Buddha himself, in MN 36, recounted years where his strength was nearly gone, and that became a doorway, not a failure. Don't restart something larger than what you can keep. Pick one habit you can actually do tomorrow, and do that.
weekly_summary.framing.honest: An honest week — about {pct}% completion. Half the time you showed up, half you didn't. Both halves are data. Notice what conditions made the difference between the days that worked and the days that didn't. That noticing is more useful than any resolution.
weekly_summary.framing.strong: A strong week — about {pct}% completion. Notice this without grasping at it. The next week may be different, and that is also fine. The mind that needs strong weeks to feel okay is the same mind the practice is meant to free.
weekly_summary.trends_empty: Factor trends will appear once you've completed three or more daily diagnostics. The check-in lives in the evening reflection.
_section_character_detail_modal: character_detail modal — detail view for CHARACTERS entries, shown during setup when tapping a character. Name/title/bio/ability/abilityEffect/abilityDesc/suttaRef all come from CHARACTERS data (content/characters/*.json).
_section_feedback_fab_menu_modal: feedback_fab_menu modal — the two-option picker shown when the floating feedback button is tapped.
feedback_fab.heading: How do you want to report?
feedback_fab.subtitle: Pick the option that fits what you want to say.
feedback_fab.classic_description: A guided form for bugs, ideas, or general feedback. Pick the area(s), write what you want to say, and the tool drafts an email you can review and send.
feedback_fab.element_description: Any UI element becomes tappable to report. Each report captures the exact code-path (e.g. today.daily_teaching_card) so the developer jumps straight to it. Copy-paste ready for pasting into a dev chat.
_section_pause_quest_modal: pause_quest modal — reached from the pause link. Records a reason; streak/shadow preserved across the pause.
pause_quest.heading: Pause the path
pause_quest.subtitle: Retreat, illness, travel — pause is part of the path
pause_quest.body_para1: While paused, the Shadow does not climb. Missed days during the pause do not count against you. Your streak is preserved across the gap. The path waits for you to return.
pause_quest.body_para2: There is no maximum pause length and no penalty for pausing. The mechanic exists because life and practice both demand pauses sometimes — a retreat where the app is rightly set aside, an illness, a death in the family. Pause when you need to. Resume when you can.
pause_quest.reason.retreat.label: Retreat
pause_quest.reason.retreat.sub: On a meditation retreat — no app needed
pause_quest.reason.illness.label: Illness or recovery
pause_quest.reason.travel.label: Travel or family
pause_quest.reason.other.label: Other reason
pause_quest.reason.other.sub: No explanation needed — pause is allowed
_section_resume_quest_modal: resume_quest modal — welcome-back screen shown when the practitioner comes back from pause. Duration + reason (by reason id) interpolated into subtitle.
resume_quest.heading: Welcome back
resume_quest.body_para1: The Shadow has not climbed during your pause. Your streak is preserved. The path picks up where you left off.
resume_quest.body_para2: Whatever you sat with on retreat, in illness, in life away from this tool — bring some of it back. The next sit can be the smallest possible. One breath of real attention is enough to begin again.
_section_tisikkha_explainer_modal: tisikkha_explainer modal — explains the three trainings (sīla / samādhi / paññā) and how the rank-gate thresholds work. Heavy canonical content referencing AN 3.88 and SN 22.45.
tisikkha.heading: Tisikkhā — the three trainings
tisikkha.subtitle: AN 3.88 · the canonical structure of the path
tisikkha.intro_para: The Buddha taught the path as three trainings that support one another. Sīla — virtue — is the foundation. On that foundation, samādhi — concentration — becomes possible. From a settled mind, paññā — wisdom — arises. The order is not optional and the support is mutual: wisdom in turn refines virtue, which deepens samādhi, which clarifies wisdom further. They grow together.
tisikkha.intro_note: Sīla and samādhi are lived — they reflect what is alive in you right now and erode when not maintained. Paññā is the layer underneath — once seen clearly, the wisdom is yours, and lifetime paññā cannot be lost.
tisikkha.sila.name: Sīla
tisikkha.sila.body: Earned by completing reflections and walking meditation. Sīla is the ground — keeping the precepts, doing no harm, supporting others. It is lived: virtue not practiced erodes. There is no preserved lifetime version because what was virtuous yesterday does not protect what is unvirtuous today.
tisikkha.samadhi.name: Samādhi
tisikkha.samadhi.body: Earned primarily through sitting. Samādhi is the unification of mind that allows phenomena to be seen clearly. It too is lived: concentration not maintained dissipates, like a lamp that goes out when the wick is no longer trimmed. No lifetime version — only what is alive in you right now.
tisikkha.panna.name: Paññā
tisikkha.panna.body_spendable: Earned by sits, journal entries, and especially by naming hindrances precisely as they arise. Paññā is also spendable — it costs paññā to read suttas above your current rank.
tisikkha.panna.body_lifetime: Paññā alone has a lifetime total that cannot be reduced. The tradition holds that the wisdom that has seen clearly is acala — unshakeable. What you have actually understood through real practice is yours, even when sīla lapses or samādhi dissipates. The current balance is what is spendable; the lifetime total is what cannot be taken.
tisikkha.threshold.endpoint: at the game's endpoint — no further threshold
tisikkha.decay.eyebrow: A note on accumulation and decay
tisikkha.decay.body: All three slowly decay if unused — about 1 per balance per 7 days. Sīla erodes when not lived. Samādhi dissipates when not maintained. Even the current paññā balance fades when wisdom is not used in practice — but the lifetime paññā remains as a record of what cannot be lost.
_section_liberation_offer_modal: liberation_offer modal — endpoint modal shown when a practitioner reaches Rank 9. Asks whether to accept the game's completion. Quotes DN 16 — Buddha's last words. Canonical hot spot for localization.
liberation_offer.eyebrow: The game has reached its end
liberation_offer.heading: Do you wish to be liberated?
liberation_offer.body_para1: You have reached the arahant rank in the game. This is the game's endpoint, not a claim about your actual stage of awakening. The game has taught what it can teach. What remains is real practice with a real teacher — the part no tracker can do for you.
liberation_offer.body_intro2: If you accept liberation now, the game will:
liberation_offer.bullet_summary: Generate a final teaching summary — a printed guideline of everything you learned, your practice history, and explicit guidance for continuing without the game
liberation_offer.bullet_mark_complete: Mark your path as complete — no further rank advancements
liberation_offer.body_summary_note: The teaching summary will be downloaded automatically. Keep it. Share it with a teacher if you have one. It is the scaffolding of the game compressed into a document you can carry into real practice.
liberation_offer.decline_note: If you are not ready, you can decline and keep sitting. The game will not advance your rank further — rank 9 is the ceiling — but nothing else changes. You can accept liberation later, any time, from the path tab.
liberation_offer.dn16_quote: "Conditioned things are of a nature to decay; strive on with diligence."
_section_liberation_complete_modal: liberation_complete modal — shown after the practitioner accepts liberation. Ends with 'Appamādena sampādetha' — the Pāli version of the Buddha's exhortation from DN 16. Canonical hot spot for localization.
liberation_complete.heading: The game is complete
liberation_complete.body_para1: Your final teaching summary has been downloaded. It is the printed guideline of everything you learned through the game, your practice history, and explicit guidance for continuing without this tool.
liberation_complete.body_para2: There is nothing more the game has to say. The scaffolding is down. What remains is your daily sit, your journal, your relationships with your teachers, and the long slow work that was always the real thing.
liberation_complete.body_para3: If you ever want to re-download the summary or review your practice history, everything is still here in the path tab. But you do not need to come back. The game has finished its job.
_section_teaching_detail_modal: teaching_detail modal — the sub-typed catch-all used from Wisdom, Path, and elsewhere to drill into a specific teaching entity. Seven sub-types: hindrance / factor / army / rank / layer / ladder / foundation_satipatthana. Content-heavy: most of the hindrance branch and all of foundation_satipatthana reference canonical sources (MN 10, MN 20, MN 118, SN 46.51, AN 5.161, AN 7.58). Translators using established doctrinal renderings should work through en.json rather than paraphrase from English.
teaching_detail.hindrance.subtitle_suffi: {english} · one of the Five Hindrances · MN 10 · AN 5.51
teaching_detail.hindrance.practice_note: When {pali} arises in a sit, name it silently — "{pali} is present" — and watch it as weather, not as you. This single practice is the foundation of Layer 1 of the path.
teaching_detail.hindrance.sensual: The pull of pleasure — food, screens, attention, comfort. In the sit, it appears as a restless reaching toward anything other than the present object. The Buddha's counsel (MN 20, SN 46.51) is to notice the pull, see the danger in following it, and starve it by attending elsewhere. Not to fight it — just not to feed it.
teaching_detail.hindrance.illwill: Irritation, resentment, the silent "no." The opposite is metta — wishing well. The classical practice is to begin with someone easy, then include someone neutral, then — and this is where the training happens — someone difficult. See AN 5.161 and the Karanīya Metta Sutta.
teaching_detail.hindrance.sloth: Heaviness, dullness, the mind closing rather than opening. Not ordinary tiredness — sloth has a quality of giving up. The Buddha's prescription in AN 7.58: brighten the mind with light, open the eyes, stand up and walk, splash water on the face, recollect the teaching. Do something, then return to the sit.
teaching_detail.hindrance.restless: Uddhacca-kukkucca — agitation and worry. The modern mind is industrially cultivated to be restless. The Buddha's direct method (MN 20 — Vitakkasaṇṭhāna) is a sequence: replace the restless thought with a calmer object; notice the danger in following it; not attend to it; examine its root; and as a last resort, use effort of will — this is the one hindrance where a little force is appropriate.
teaching_detail.hindrance.doubt: Vicikicchā — not healthy questioning but the paralyzing kind. The food that starves doubt (SN 46.51) is two things: wise attention, and time with kalyāṇamittā — good friends who have walked further than you.
teaching_detail.factor.subtitle_suffix: {english} · one of the Seven Awakening Factors · {suttaRef}
teaching_detail.factor.measurable_body: Measured from {derivation}. Visible as a live score on the Path tab.
teaching_detail.factor.nonmeasurable_bod: Not directly measurable. Emerges in practice when {when}. Marked on the Path tab only when the conditions are visible.
teaching_detail.factor.sequence_note: The factors arise in sequence. Each one supports the next. You cannot skip ahead.
teaching_detail.army.canonical_framing_l: The work, in the canonical framing
teaching_detail.army.canonical_framing_b: The armies are not defeated quickly. They weaken through sustained practice over long timeframes. Each is faced one at a time — this is how the Buddha himself worked with them under the Bodhi tree. Layer 2 of the game's path tracks your engagement with them, one at a time, after Layer 1's gate has held on at least one day.
teaching_detail.ladder.title: The ten ranks
teaching_detail.ladder.subtitle: The full canonical ladder · Visuddhimagga + MN 22 · AN 4.241
teaching_detail.ladder.intro: Ten ranks, from uninstructed worldling to fully awakened. Ranks 0–5 are the preliminary work — the kalyāṇa-puthujjana stages and the approach to the stream. Ranks 6–9 are the canonical noble persons, achievable in the game as teaching milestones but explicitly not claimed as real attainments. The game terminates at rank 9 with a liberation ritual that hands you a final teaching summary and points you out of the tool.
teaching_detail.foundation.body.title: Kāyānupassanā — mindfulness of body
teaching_detail.foundation.body.body:
Mindfulness of body is the most concrete of the four foundations — and usually the first the Buddha taught. It has several sub-practices: mindfulness of posture (sitting, standing, walking, lying down); mindfulness of breathing (ānāpānasati, MN 118 — the complete sixteen-step practice that can carry a practitioner all the way); mindfulness of the body's parts; and mindfulness of the four elements. The breath is the most common entry because it is always present and always neutral.

In MN 118 the Buddha gives the sixteen-step ānāpānasati as a complete path. The first four steps train the body: long breath, short breath, sensitivity to the whole body, calming the body. The next four train feeling, the next four train mind, the last four train wisdom. The whole path through the breath alone.
teaching_detail.foundation.feeling.title: Vedanānupassanā — mindfulness of feeling tone
teaching_detail.foundation.feeling.body:
Feeling tone (vedanā) is the second foundation and often the most underdeveloped in modern practitioners. It means noticing whether each sensation, as it arises, is pleasant, unpleasant, or neutral — before the story about the sensation starts. This is not the same as mood or emotion; it is the raw hedonic tone that precedes them.

The practice is to watch, for a stretch of the sit, every arising experience with one question only: pleasant, unpleasant, or neutral? Do not name the content. Just the tone. This trains the mind to catch feeling tone before craving or aversion latches on.
teaching_detail.foundation.mind.title: Cittānupassanā — mindfulness of mind
teaching_detail.foundation.mind.body:
Mindfulness of mind is the third foundation. It means turning attention to the quality of mind itself — is it contracted or spacious? Agitated or settled? Clinging or releasing? Concentrated or scattered? Greedy or free? Angry or open? Deluded or clear? The mind becomes its own object.

This foundation is sometimes skipped because it feels abstract, but it is accessible: every time you catch yourself thinking "oh, the mind is distracted right now" or "the mind is bright right now," you are doing cittānupassanā.
teaching_detail.foundation.dhammas.title: Dhammānupassanā — mindfulness of phenomena
teaching_detail.foundation.dhammas.body:
The fourth foundation, dhammānupassanā, is the most subtle. It means watching mental phenomena as they arise and pass — specifically: the five hindrances (are they present or absent?), the five aggregates, the six sense bases, the seven awakening factors (are they strong or weak?), and the four noble truths as they apply to actual experience.

This foundation is where the teaching of the path and the experience of the practice meet. Every time you notice "sensual desire is present" or "sati is arising," you are doing dhammānupassanā. It is also the foundation most directly connected to Layer 1 of the game's path — the gate requires you to be doing this work.
_section_guided_flow_modal: meditation_tutorial / guided_flow modal — shared render branch for GUIDED_FLOWS.{id}. Flow title, step titles, step bodies come from data (content/guided-flows/*.json). Only chrome lives here: progress pill, back/next/finish buttons.
_section_rules_card_modal: rules_card modal — the game-mechanics summary. Reached from the 'see all game rules →' link in tisikkha_explainer and shadow_explainer. Dense bullet-list of game economy (what earns what, what raises/recedes the Shadow, decay rules). Each bullet is a full static key — attempted item-level subdivision would fragment the rendered sentence.
rules_card.heading: The game's rules
rules_card.subtitle: What earns what · what protects what · what cannot be lost
rules_card.samadhi_earn.item_walking: Walking meditation — +1 samādhi (in the same earn package)
rules_card.samadhi_earn.item_reflections: Deep & open reflections build samādhi by closing the day attentively
rules_card.panna_earn.item_hindrance: Naming a hindrance precisely — +1 paññā each (capped at +3 per entry)
rules_card.panna_earn.item_sutta: Reading a sutta at or below your level — +1 paññā
rules_card.panna_permanent.body: Paññā cannot be taken from you by any Shadow event, missed sit, broken streak, or setback. The wisdom you have actually earned through real practice is yours. Even if you stop practicing for months and the Shadow grows to its maximum, your paññā balance stays intact. The slow 7-day exercise decay still applies — wisdom not used in practice fades — but only deliberate spending (for sutta access) and that slow trickle can reduce it. SN 22.45: the stream-enterer's wisdom faculty is acala — unshakeable.
rules_card.shadow_raises.item_missed: Missed scheduled sit (after the time has passed)
rules_card.shadow_raises.item_low: Day with low practice score and key habits missed
rules_card.shadow_raises.item_hindrance: Hindrance composite spiking in the daily diagnostic
rules_card.shadow_raises.item_above_rank: Reaching for suttas above your current rank (small tick)
rules_card.shadow_recedes.item_gate: A day where the gate's three legs hold
rules_card.shadow_floor.body: No matter how much you practice, the Shadow cannot drop below the floor for your current rank. Māra is always present. The floor lowers only when you advance ranks, and even at rank 9 it does not reach zero entirely. See the Shadow explainer for the full curve.
rules_card.decay.body: Sīla, samādhi, and paññā each lose 1 per balance per 7 days unused. Lifetime totals never decrease. The principle: virtue not practiced erodes, concentration not maintained dissipates, wisdom not used in practice fades.
_section_privacy_detail_modal: privacy_detail modal — plain-language privacy note. Each paragraph lives as a single key with inline <b> for the heading portion, rather than splitting heading/body; the paragraphs are atomic in reading order and German/Malay sentence structure may put the heading inline rather than at the start.
privacy_detail.heading: How your data is handled
privacy_detail.subtitle: Plain language. No fine print.
privacy_detail.para_where: Where your data lives. Everything you do in this app — diagnostic answers, reflections, journal entries, which habits you've completed, your character choices, anything you've typed — is stored in your browser's local storage on this specific device. Nothing else.
privacy_detail.para_not_sent: What is NOT sent anywhere. By default, the app uses no account, no server, no database, no analytics, no tracking pixels, no third-party scripts that phone home. Your reflections stay in your browser. The maintainer cannot read your journal entries because the maintainer has no way to.
privacy_detail.para_sync_optional: Optional cross-device sync. You can sign in with email and password to sync your practice across devices. When you do, you set a second secret — an encryption passphrase, separate from your account password. Your practice data is encrypted in your browser with a key derived from that passphrase, and only the ciphertext is uploaded to the server. The passphrase itself never leaves your device. Your account password is resettable by email; the passphrase is not. If you forget it, your synced data cannot be recovered by anyone — not by Supabase, not by the maintainer. That is the honest cost of end-to-end encryption.
privacy_detail.para_sync_technical: The technical details. Your passphrase is stretched through PBKDF2 with SHA-256 and 600,000 iterations (per OWASP 2026 password-storage guidance) together with a random 16-byte salt to derive a non-extractable AES-GCM-256 key. Each save uses a fresh random 12-byte IV. Ciphertext is base64-encoded and stored in a Supabase row whose row-level-security policies restrict read and write to your authenticated user id. The derived key lives only in your tab's memory — it is never written to disk, never logged, never transmitted. All cryptography runs in your browser via the built-in Web Crypto API.
privacy_detail.para_sync_references: Verify for yourself. Adze's source code is public at github.com/slate915first/adze — read src/systems/passphrase.js and src/systems/crypto.js for the crypto primitives. Reference specs: Web Crypto API (MDN), AES-GCM (NIST SP 800-38D), PBKDF2 (RFC 2898), OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet.
privacy_detail.para_outbound: The only outbound actions. Two things leave your device, and both require your explicit tap. First: when you click a SuttaCentral link, your browser loads a page from suttacentral.net in a new tab — standard web behavior, same as clicking any link anywhere. Second: when you choose to send beta feedback, your mail client opens a draft with app version, current screen, and basic device info pre-filled. You can edit or delete any of that before sending. Nothing leaves until you press send in your mail app.
privacy_detail.para_tailwind: Third-party scripts: none. Every script the page needs (Tailwind for styling, the Supabase JS SDK for sync) is vendored locally under /vendor/. Your browser never fetches code from a third-party CDN at runtime, so your IP address is not transmitted to any third party just by opening the page.
privacy_detail.para_clear: What happens if you clear browser data. Everything goes. The app cannot recover it for you because it never had a copy anywhere else. This is a trade-off: true privacy on one side, no cross-device backup on the other. If you care about keeping your reflections, use the Export button in Settings regularly and save the JSON file somewhere you trust.
privacy_detail.para_others: What happens if someone else uses your browser profile. They can open the app and see your data. Local storage is not encrypted. If privacy from others using your device matters, use a private browser profile, a passworded OS account, or open the app only in incognito mode (but be aware incognito mode clears on close).
privacy_detail.para_gdpr: GDPR / DSGVO summary. In anonymous mode no third party processes your data. With sync enabled, Adze (controller) and three EU-SCC-covered US processors (Supabase, Cloudflare, Resend) handle your data — but your practice content is encrypted in your browser before it leaves, so the processors only see ciphertext. Your full GDPR rights apply. The formal Art. 13 legal notice — controller details, every purpose with its lawful basis, recipients, retention windows, supervisory-authority contact — is in the Detailed legal notice linked below.
