privacy_detail.para_cookies: Cookies. The app sets no cookies. The browser storage it uses is localStorage, which is a separate mechanism — it stays on your device and is never transmitted in HTTP headers the way cookies can be.
privacy_detail.para_export: The data you can export. In Settings → Backup & Share → Export, you get a plain JSON file of everything. You can open this in any text editor to read it, move it between devices, or keep a backup. Importing a file replaces whatever is currently stored.
privacy_detail.para_destroy: If you want your data destroyed. Settings → Reset Everything wipes the local storage for this app on this device. That is all that exists; once that is gone, the data is gone.
privacy_detail.footer: If you notice the app doing something that contradicts this note, that is a bug — please send a [HQ-BUG] report via the feedback button. This note is an honest description of how the app is designed to work.
_section_struggle_suggestion_modal: struggle_suggestion modal — Buddha-pointed-at-this response to a reflection entry that names a struggle. Two framings depending on whether the suggested sutta has already been read.
struggle_suggestion.framing_already: In what you wrote, I heard something the Buddha spoke directly to. You have already read this — sometimes a teaching needs to be returned to. The layer beneath the first reading is often where the answer waits.
struggle_suggestion.framing_new: In what you wrote, I heard something the Buddha spoke directly to. If you are ready, here is what he taught.
struggle_suggestion.footer_note: Practice is more important than reading. The suggestion is only a pointer.
_section_sutta_subcategory_modal: sutta_subcategory modal — list of suttas tagged to a specific practical topic (e.g. anger, doubt, grief). SUTTA_SUBCATEGORIES provides the label/icon/teaser per topic from data.
sutta_subcategory.empty_body: No suttas are yet tagged for this topic. The library is growing.
_section_sutta_view_modal: sutta_view modal — per-sutta detail view. Four action-block branches (justRead overreach / justRead normal / already-read / ready / above-rank) each rendered from its own static key set (lesson #3, #8). References canonical kāmacchanda teaching in the overreach body.
sutta_view.summary_note: This is a short paraphrase to help you decide whether to read the original. The actual sutta is in the canon — Bhikkhu Bodhi's translations are recommended. The summary is not the teaching; the sutta itself is.
sutta_view.topic_link_note: Tap any topic to find related teachings
sutta_view.sc.footer: Opens in a new tab. Texts and translations via SuttaCentral. Where Bhikkhu Bodhi's published translation exists, it is used; otherwise Bhante Sujato's free translation is used (as with most DN and some Sutta Nipāta texts).
sutta_view.marked_read_overreach: You read above your rank. The reach itself was driven in part by sensual craving for advanced understanding — this is how kāmacchanda appears in subtle form. Your paññā balance has been reduced by {cost}, and today's sensual-desire diagnostic was ticked by +{tick}. None of this is punishment. It is the canonical feedback that reaching beyond the foundation has a cost. Read again when you are ready.
sutta_view.marked_read_normal: +1 paññā. The reading at or below your rank is the canonical mode of study — neither too early nor too late. Look up the original sutta in your translation when you have time.
sutta_view.already_read_body: You have read this sutta before. Returning to it is always available; only first reads contribute to paññā.
sutta_view.ready_body: You are at rank {rank}, and this sutta is for rank {minRank}+. Reading earns +1 paññā.
sutta_view.above_rank_body: This sutta is for {pali} (rank {minRank}). You are at rank {rank}. The mind that reaches for advanced teachings before the foundation is held often does so because of subtle craving for attainment. Notice. Then read if you still wish.
sutta_view.above_rank_cost_sufficient: Reading above your rank costs {cost} paññā and ticks today's sensual-desire diagnostic by +{tick}. You currently have {have} paññā.
sutta_view.above_rank_cost_insufficient: Reading above your rank costs {cost} paññā and ticks today's sensual-desire diagnostic by +{tick}. You currently have {have} paññā — not enough, but the read is allowed anyway because the dhamma is not gatekept.
sutta_view.study.intro_body: This sutta has {total} comprehension questions broken into its structural pieces. Studying them with spaced repetition is how the tradition has always turned reading into understanding. The cards take about 30 seconds each; what you already know stays visible only occasionally, what is shaky comes back soon.
sutta_view.study.all_scheduled: All cards scheduled. Next review surfaces automatically when due.
_section_render_quest: renderQuest — the 'your quest' summary tab showing the 5-stage Bodhisatta-path structure. Stage names/subtitles/descriptions come from STAGES data (content/dhamma/stages.json). Only chrome lives here.
quest.no_active: No guided path active. You are walking the Free Path.
quest.subtitle: Modeled on the Bodhisatta's path through the suttas
quest.stage_locked_note: Locked — complete previous stages to reveal
quest.key_habit_days: {current} / {required} days of full key habit completion
_section_render_teachings_section: renderTeachingsSection — the block rendered below the quest card on the Quest tab. Lists GUIDED_FLOWS entries (data) and two static entry buttons (Foundations, Hindrance Library).
teachings.foundations_body: The Four Noble Truths and the Eightfold Path. The structural top of everything the Buddha taught. Begin here when you don't know where to begin.
teachings.foundations_tap_to_read: Tap to read — recommended for new practitioners
teachings.library_body: Browse the seven sutta-grounded teachings on the hindrances and the resources of the path. Available regardless of diagnostic.
_section_render_sangha: renderSangha — multi-practitioner view. Member card shows rank, focus, dominant hindrance, sit counts. Collapsible intro with SN 45.2 (admirable friendship) teaching.
sangha.heading: 🪷 The Sangha
sangha.collapsed_sub_solo: You walk alone for now. Tap to read what a real sangha is and why this tab is honestly not one yet.
sangha.collapsed_sub_group: Where each companion stands on the path. Tap to expand.
sangha.expanded_solo_para1: A sangha, in the suttas, is the community of those walking the path together. In its truest sense, the Buddha meant the noble sangha — those who have realized at least sotāpatti. In its everyday sense, it means the people you practice with: a teacher, a dhamma friend, a partner, a local group.
sangha.expanded_solo_para2: This tab, honestly, is not a sangha yet. It is a mirror of your own walk — your rank, your focus, your current hindrance. A real sangha is made of people, not data. A later version of this tool will allow inviting a partner, teacher, or dhamma friend to share this space so you can support each other across devices. That system is being built carefully and is not ready.
sangha.expanded_solo_para3: Your real sangha, starting today: the teacher you read or listen to (Ajahn Chah, Bhikkhu Bodhi, whoever you trust); one living practitioner you can talk to — your partner, a friend, someone from a local group; and the suttas themselves. The Buddha was clear in SN 45.2: "Admirable friendship, admirable companionship, admirable association — this is the whole of the holy life." No app replaces that. This one is honest about it.
sangha.expanded_group_intro: Where each companion stands on the path. Not a leaderboard — a shared view, so you can support each other in the work.
sangha.privacy_note: Each companion's data is stored locally on this device only. Nothing is uploaded. If you want to share where you are with a teacher or visiting friend, use the export below — it includes your rank, gate status, hindrance severities, factor scores, and army states, but never any of your reflection text.
sangha.member.no_dominant: No dominant hindrance over the last 14 days — keep watching.
sangha.footer_solo: The Buddha himself walked alone for years before others joined him. It is harder, but it is honorable. When you are ready for companions on the path — actual people, not characters — the Sangha tab will hold them here.
sangha.footer_group: "Admirable friendship, admirable companionship, admirable association is the whole of the holy life." — SN 45.2, the Buddha to Ānanda. When one companion struggles, the rest can carry the practice for them with their own.
_section_render_today: renderToday — the daily Today view, the highest-traffic surface in the app. Contains banners (primary alert / bump offer / downgrade offer / stage preview / sustained advance / paused), the Ajahn Chah teaching card with source-dependent headers, the Next Step + quote two-column hero, the pre-sit intention inset, the three-column Meditation/Reading/Reflection grid, and the intention readback. Dense with canonical references — anupubbasikkhā (gradual training), Padhāna Sutta Sn 3.2 (five-hindrance-to-army mapping), and Bodhisatta-path consistency teachings.
today.no_habits.body: No habits yet. Add some to begin!
today.sustained_advance.body: Day {day} — the three-legged gate held today. The counter advances by one.
today.primary_alert.monthly_sub: 15 minutes when you can sit without interruption →
today.primary_alert.weekly_summary_sub: Seven days of data. Look back without grading yourself →
_section_bump_offer: Bump offer banner — the 'gradual training' growth suggestion after 7 unbroken days. Canonical reference: anupubbasikkhā, the Buddha's principle of gradual training by one supportable step. Translators: anupubbasikkhā may be preserved as Pāli or translated via the tradition's established rendering.
today.bump.eyebrow: A gentle offer, not a demand
today.bump.body: Seven days of unbroken practice. The Buddha called this anupubbasikkhā — gradual training, one supportable step at a time. If today feels steady, the minutes can grow a little. If today feels full already, hold what you have — a steady practice is worth more than a stretched one.
today.bump.footer_note: No penalty either way. If declined, the offer returns after another seven perfect days.
_section_downgrade_offer: Downgrade offer banner — the 'consistency over duration' compassionate right-sizing. Canonical reference: the Buddha's teaching that an unbroken thread of small practice is stronger than a sustained attempt that keeps breaking.
today.downgrade.body: The practice has been hard this week — {misses} misses in {windowDays} days. The Buddha was clear: an unbroken thread of a small sit is stronger than an hour that keeps breaking. Dropping to a length you can actually hold is honorable. Keeping the current length and trying again is also honorable.
today.downgrade.footer_note: No penalty either way. If kept at current level, the offer returns in a week if the pattern continues.
_section_teaching_card: Ajahn Chah (and friends) daily-teaching card. Five source-dependent header variants — static key per branch. Quote source field comes from data (AJAHN_CHAH_TEACHINGS and related).
today.teaching.save_tooltip: Save this teaching to your collection (viewable in Review tab)
today.teaching.saved_tooltip: Saved — tap to remove. View all saved quotes in the Review tab.
today.teaching.matched_tooltip: Matched to your dominant hindrance over the last 14 days
today.paused.body_one: On {reason} for 1 day — Shadow held, streak preserved
today.paused.body_many: On {reason} for {n} days — Shadow held, streak preserved
_section_intention_inset: Pre-sit intention inset — set inside the Next Step card when the next step is a sit. Names the dominant hindrance AND the Māra army from the Padhāna Sutta (Sn 3.2) that corresponds to it. Canonical hot spot: the Padhāna Sutta's ten-army framework.
today.intention.no_dominant: No dominant hindrance over the last 14 days — observe what arises.
today.intention.footer_note: The reflection at the end of the day will check whether you noticed.
_section_today_meditation_items: Meditation column items. Classified habits get subtitles per category (sit / walking / metta / meditation). If the practitioner doesn't have metta or walking configured, they appear as faded suggestions.
today.med.suggestion_sub: add as a habit in settings
_section_today_reading_items: Reading column items. Foundation = the current weekly foundation reading; sutta of the day = a hindrance-matched sutta at or below the practitioner's rank.
today.refl.reflection_sub_default: a line tonight — deeper if you have the energy
today.refl.evening_sub_undone: two questions, then go as deep as you have energy for
today.intention_readback.with_observe: You set out to cultivate {cultivate} and observe {pali} ({english}). Carry this through the day. The reflection will check whether either arose clearly.
today.intention_readback.no_observe: You set out to cultivate {cultivate}. Carry this through the day. The reflection will check whether either arose clearly.
_section_evening_close_modal: evening_close — the four-phase Pre-Dashboard evening ceremony. Phases: gauge (pick depth based on energy/time) → minimal/standard/deep/open (chosen depth) → done (close the day). Canonical hot spot: DN 16 paraphrase at end ('Conditioned things are of a nature to decay; strive on with diligence' — the Buddha's last words).
evening_close.intent_readback.with_obser: You set out to cultivate {cultivate} and observe {pali}. As you close the day — did either arise clearly? Hold this lightly through the reflection.
evening_close.intent_readback.no_observe: You set out to cultivate {cultivate}. As you close the day — did this arise clearly? Hold this lightly through the reflection.
_section_evening_close_oneline: evening_close.oneline — v15.15 merged entry phase. A one-line capture with two exits: save+rest (counts as full daily) or save+go-deeper (continues into the existing 6-phase flow).
evening_close.oneline.heading: Today in one line.
evening_close.oneline.subtitle: A single sentence is enough. Keep what matters.
evening_close.oneline.hindrance_hint: If a hindrance named itself, you can name it here — it counts toward the path either way.
evening_close.oneline.footer_hint: Either choice closes the day. The deeper path is optional, never owed.
evening_close.oneline.sliders_intro: Before you write — a quick read of tonight's hindrance landscape:
evening_close.gauge.heading: Close the day
evening_close.gauge.subtitle: First — what do you have for this?
evening_close.gauge.depth_desc_minimal: Three quick taps. Honor the tiredness.
evening_close.gauge.depth_desc_standard: A few short questions. The middle path.
evening_close.gauge.depth_desc_deep: A guided self-survey across the four foundations.
evening_close.gauge.depth_desc_open: No prescribed shape — write into the day as long as it serves.
evening_close.gauge.rest_button: Take rest for today — the practice continues tomorrow
evening_close.minimal.heading: Close in 90 seconds
evening_close.minimal.subtitle: Three taps. Then sleep.
evening_close.standard.heading: Standard close
evening_close.standard.subtitle: Three short questions, then the day rests.
evening_close.standard.q3_placeholder: What was alive today? What was hard? What helped?
evening_close.deep.heading: Go deeper
evening_close.deep.subtitle: A guided survey across the four foundations.
evening_close.deep.foundation_body_desc: How present was attention to the body today?
evening_close.deep.foundation_feeling_de: Did you catch the pleasant/unpleasant/neutral before the story?
evening_close.deep.foundation_mind_desc: Did you notice the quality of mind itself? Spacious, contracted...?
evening_close.deep.foundation_dhammas_de: Did you see hindrances arising and passing? Awakening factors?
evening_close.deep.reflection_placeholde: What did you notice across the foundations? What was the texture of today's practice?
evening_close.open.heading: Sit with the whole day
evening_close.open.subtitle: No prescribed shape. Take as long as it serves.
evening_close.open.contemplation_placeho: Write into the day. The hindrances that came. What you saw. What was honest. What you would say to a teacher about today's practice. Take your time.
evening_close.open.footer_note: When you are done, the page will quiet. Until then, write.
evening_close.done.heading: The day is held
_canonical_dn16_last_words: DN 16 Buddha's last words — canonical hot spot. Translators: preserve the teaching exactly. 'Conditioned things...' is Walshe's rendering of vayadhammā saṅkhārā appamādena sampādethā.
evening_close.done.dn16_quote: Conditioned things are of a nature to decay; strive on with diligence.
evening_close.suggestion.framing_already: In what you wrote, I heard something the Buddha spoke directly to. You have already read this sutta — and sometimes a teaching needs to be returned to. The layer beneath the first reading is often where the answer is waiting.
evening_close.suggestion.framing_new: In what you wrote, I heard something the Buddha spoke directly to. If you are ready, here is what he taught.
evening_close.suggestion.dismiss_note: You can dismiss this — the closing matters more than the recommendation. Practice is more important than reading.

habit_manager.heading: Habit Manager
habit_manager.advice: 💡 Adding too many habits at once is the most common reason habit systems fail. Stick with what your path gave you for the first stage. Add more only after the key habit holds.
habit_manager.key_locked_alert: Cannot delete the key habit while the path is active. The key habit is the foundation of the path.
_section_settings: renderSettings — the settings page. Has subsections: overview, habit config link, bell sound, timer mode, points display, feedback entry points, privacy summary, backup & share, danger zone.
settings.overview.heading: ⚙️ Settings
settings.habits_config.heading: 📋 Habits configuration
settings.habits_config.body: Add, remove, or reschedule the habits you track. The dashboard shows the configured habits as today's practice — this is where you set them up.
settings.bell.heading: 🔔 Bell sound
settings.bell.body: The bell rings at the start, halfway point, and end of every sit. Choose the texture that fits your tradition or feels right to your ear. Tap preview to hear each one.
settings.timer.heading: 🧘 Meditation timer
settings.timer.body: When you tap a sit habit to mark it done, what should happen?
settings.timer.ask_body: Offer the timer, with a skip option if you used your own.
settings.timer.always_body: Go straight into the bell and the sit.
settings.timer.never_title: Never ask — just mark it done
settings.timer.never_body: For practitioners who use their own timer.
settings.points.heading: 🔢 Points display
settings.points.body: Some practitioners find that visible point values on each habit invite subtle scoreboarding — the mind starts sitting for points rather than for presence. If so, hide them.
settings.points.show_body: Visible on each habit row and in the day's score.
settings.points.hide_body: The game still tracks completion and tisikkhā internally. The display simply stops showing numbers so the practice is not scoreboarded.
settings.theme.heading: 🪷 Visual intensity
settings.theme.body: The classic theme is dramatic — violet night, glowing gold, red flare around Māra. Some practitioners find this activating rather than settling. Calm mode mutes the visual register toward a forest-tradition aesthetic: warm charcoal, dusty amber, Māra still named but no red alarm. Nothing is hidden — the shadow bar, Next Step, and banners all still render. Only the theatrical amplification is dimmed.
settings.theme.classic_body: The full visual register — violet surfaces, gold glow, red Māra, ambient breath and shimmer animations.
settings.theme.calm_body: Warm sepia surfaces, dusty amber path, muted bronze for Māra, shadow overlay dimmed to near-zero, ambient animations frozen. The game remains — only the loudness is gone.
settings.feedback.heading: 📮 Beta feedback
settings.feedback.body: Adze is in beta. Your reports shape the app. Each button opens a structured form that takes under a minute — tap the category, mark what happened, add a short comment. Nothing sends until you hit send in your mail client.
settings.feedback.log_one: You've sent 1 report from this device — thank you for contributing.
settings.feedback.log_many: You've sent {n} reports from this device — thank you for contributing.
settings.privacy.heading: 🔒 Your data, your device
settings.privacy.body: By default, everything — reflections, journal entries, diagnostic answers — lives only in this browser, on this device. If you sign in to sync across devices, your data is encrypted in the browser using a key derived from your password before it's sent to Supabase. The server stores ciphertext only; the maintainer cannot read it, and neither can Supabase.
settings.backup.heading: Backup & Share
settings.backup.body: All data is stored in your browser only. Export to back up or move between devices.
settings.danger.heading: Danger Zone
settings.danger.abandon_confirm: Leave the current path? Persistent XP and wisdom are kept. You can begin a new path when you are ready.

_section_defeat_modal: defeat modal — shadow-rollover ceremony. Softened from earlier versions; not 'defeat' in a dramatic sense, but the Bodhisatta's walking away from Āḷāra Kālāma. Canonical hot spot: SN 22.101 adze simile (already flagged earlier for path_viewer use).

defeat.subtitle: Not failure — information. The current attempt has accumulated more missed sits than it could carry. The tradition has a clear response: begin again.
defeat.sn22_101_quote: "A practitioner undertaking the development cannot see 'today my fetters wore down this much.' Like the carpenter who cannot see the daily wearing of his adze handle — but after long use sees the marks worn into the wood and knows the wearing happened. Use the adze."

defeat.keep_xp: 30% of this attempt's XP carried into your permanent total
defeat.keep_preserved: Your character, your sangha, your reflections — all preserved
defeat.begins_again: What begins again: the stage progress, the daily log. The next attempt starts a little wiser than the last. That is how the path actually moves.

_section_setback_recovery_modal: setback_recovery modal — broken-streak ceremony. Canonical hot spot: AN 6.55 Soṇa lute simile (the middle way — 'too tight or too loose, the lute will not play well').
setback_recovery.an6_55_para1: There is a story in AN 6.55. A monk named Soṇa had been practicing so hard that his feet were bloody from walking meditation. He was about to give up the path entirely. The Buddha came to him and asked: "Soṇa, when you played the lute, if the strings were too tight, did the lute play well?" "No, lord." "And when the strings were too loose, did the lute play well?" "No, lord." "But when the strings were neither too tight nor too loose — when they were tuned to just the right pitch — then the lute played well, didn't it?"
setback_recovery.an6_55_para2: "In the same way, Soṇa, over-tightened effort produces restlessness. Over-loosened effort produces sloth. Find the middle tuning. Then practice."
setback_recovery.reflection_intro: A broken streak is not a verdict on you. It is information about the tuning. Two questions worth asking, gently:
setback_recovery.question_tight: Was the commitment too tight for what your life actually contains right now?
setback_recovery.question_smaller: Is there a smaller version of this you could keep, without fail, starting tomorrow?
setback_recovery.smaller_button: Begin again at a smaller commitment
setback_recovery.hold_button: Hold the same commitment, walk on

_section_datenschutz_modal: datenschutz modal — formal GDPR Art. 13 disclosure. Companion to privacy_detail (which is the friendly plain-language overview). Currently English; German translation tracked as a follow-up. Each para_* key is one section; subtitle uses the German term so the legal nature is clear at a glance.
datenschutz.heading: Datenschutzerklärung
datenschutz.subtitle: GDPR Art. 13 disclosure — what we process, why, and your rights.
datenschutz.para_controller: 1. Controller (Verantwortlicher). Dirk Sauerstein, Bischofstr. 2 A, 04179 Leipzig, Germany. Contact: hello@adze.life. Full legal-entity details in the Impressum.
datenschutz.para_purposes:
2. Purposes and lawful bases.
(1) Authentication — your e-mail address and the magic-link code we send to it. Lawful basis: Art. 6 (1) b GDPR (contract — providing the sign-in service).
(2) Cross-device sync — your encrypted practice data (ciphertext only). Lawful basis: Art. 6 (1) b GDPR.
(3) Beta access control — your e-mail checked against the closed-beta allowlist. Lawful basis: Art. 6 (1) f GDPR (legitimate interest — preventing abuse during closed beta).
(4) Service security — server logs (IP, user agent, request timestamps) at our hosting providers. Lawful basis: Art. 6 (1) f GDPR (legitimate interest — operational security).
(5) Bug reports — when you click the in-app feedback button, your mail client opens a draft you can edit before sending. Lawful basis: Art. 6 (1) a GDPR (consent, given by sending the e-mail).
datenschutz.para_recipients: 3. Recipients and processors. Three processors under Art. 28 GDPR: Supabase Inc. (USA — auth + database hosting; ciphertext only, cannot read your practice data); Cloudflare Inc. (USA — web hosting + edge logs); Resend (USA — outbound e-mail delivery for the magic-link). All three operate under data processing agreements. No marketing or analytics partners; no advertising networks; no data brokers.
datenschutz.para_transfers: 4. Third-country transfers. All three processors are headquartered in the USA. Transfers are based on Standard Contractual Clauses (Art. 46 (2) c GDPR + EU Implementing Decision 2021/914, Module 2 — controller to processor). For your encrypted practice data, the practical transfer risk is bounded because the ciphertext cannot be decrypted by the processor.
datenschutz.para_retention:
5. Retention.
Sessions: until your sign-in token expires or you sign out (typically 1–24 hours).
Account e-mail address + encrypted practice data: until you delete your account, or 24 months of inactivity (auto-deletion will be implemented before public release; today only explicit deletion via the in-app delete-account button is active).
Beta allowlist entry: until public sign-up launches, or you ask for removal.
Hosting provider logs (Cloudflare): ~30 days (provider default).
Auth logs (Supabase): ~90 days (provider default).
Bug-report e-mails in our inbox: 24 months.
datenschutz.para_rights: 6. Your rights. You can request: access to a copy of your data (Art. 15); rectification of incorrect data (Art. 16); erasure (Art. 17 — note that synced practice data is encrypted with your passphrase, so erasing the unreadable ciphertext is straightforward); restriction of processing (Art. 18); portability of the data you provided (Art. 20 — Settings → Backup & Share → Export already gives you a JSON file); objection to processing based on legitimate interest (Art. 21). To exercise any of these, write to hello@adze.life. We respond within 30 days (Art. 12 (3)).
datenschutz.para_complaint: 7. Right to lodge a complaint. You can complain to the supervisory authority of your habitual residence, place of work, or the place of the alleged infringement (Art. 77 GDPR). For the controller's location: Sächsischer Datenschutz- und Transparenzbeauftragter, Devrientstraße 1, 01067 Dresden, Germany — saechsdsb.de.
datenschutz.para_required: 8. Required information. An e-mail address is contractually required for sign-in and cross-device sync. Without it you cannot create a synced account; you can still use Adze in fully anonymous local-only mode (no e-mail, no server, no sign-in).
datenschutz.para_no_automated: 9. No automated decision-making. We do not perform automated decision-making within the meaning of Art. 22 GDPR. The diagnostic engine that suggests a starting practice is a deterministic recommendation, not an automated decision with legal or significant effect.
datenschutz.para_cookies_position: 10. Cookies and similar storage. Adze sets no tracking cookies, no analytics scripts, no marketing pixels. The browser storage we use (localStorage) is necessary for the explicitly-requested service (saving your practice locally; preserving your sign-in session) and falls under § 25 Abs. 2 Nr. 2 TDDDG (the German implementation of the ePrivacy Directive). No consent banner is required because no consent-required storage is used.
datenschutz.para_source: 11. Source of the data. All personal data we process was provided by you — e-mail at sign-up, content you create in the app. We do not buy, scrape, or import data from third parties.
datenschutz.para_effective: 12. Effective date and changes. This notice was last updated on 19 April 2026. Changes are versioned in CHANGELOG.md. Material changes affecting how your data is processed will be communicated by e-mail to account holders before they take effect.
datenschutz.footer: If you notice the app processing data in a way that contradicts this notice, that is a bug — please report it via the in-app feedback button or to hello@adze.life.
